description: The Automatic has been a staple design and backbone of our high-performance shortboard shapes.. A constant refinement from Dan Boehne the design continues to evolve. A fine-tuned rocker curve with an extra flip in the tail. Deep single concave and thin foil make this the go-to design for good waves. Hollow waves, point waves, airs, finners, barrels are all in the arsenal. This is our most popular high-performance design. We also offer the Automatic in a smaller wave step down version to complete the competition quiver. Also we now offer in performance grom sizes (both versions custom order only) Below is a list of standard Dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust dims if you want to get specific with the design. Hollow waves, point waves, airs, finners, barrels are all in the arsenal. This is our most popular high-performance design. We also offer the Automatic in a smaller wave step down version to complete the competition quiver. Also we now offer in performance grom sizes (both versions custom order only) Below is a list of standard Dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust dims if you want to get specific with the design. Hollow waves, point waves, airs, finners, barrels are all in the arsenal. This is our most popular high-performance design. We also offer the Automatic in a smaller wave step down version to complete the competition quiver. Also we now offer in performance grom sizes (both versions custom order only) Below is a list of standard Dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust dims if you want to get specific with the design. description: Albee Layer is a rad human. His big wave game is on another level and he's breaking boundaries in the air on a daily basis. We are so honored to work with such a talented surfer. 2016 was a big one for Mr. Layer as he walked away with 2 Surfer Poll Awards including, Best Maneuver for his backside double spin. Not everyone can agree if it was a 720 or 900 but what everyone can agree on is that is was the first of it's kind with a Dan Boehne shape under his feet. This board is a direct influence of today's modern-day surfing antics. Designed by Dan Boehne and Albee, tested in Maui and beyond. The Albee is a step-down shortboard that packs as much volume as they could get into it without falling into the fish category. It is specifically designed for modern air style surfing with the ability to hold the rail in overhead surf. Albee rides his 4" shorter than he is. Standard Dims: 5'6 x 19.25 x 2.38 x 26.75L 5'8 x 19.38 x 2.44 x 28.32L 5'9 x 19.5 x 2.5 x 29.55L 5'10 x 19.63 x 2.56 x 30.87L (Albee's size) 5'11 x 19.88 x 2.6 x 32.10L 6'0 x 20 x 2.63 x 33.11L

description: This board is a design collaboration between Albee Layer and Dan Boehne. Ever since we've been working with Albee on boards, the efforts have been to ride smaller more compact minimalist performance boards. Albee is a hybrid in the way he surfs as he is an influential aerial surfer as well likes to lay it on a rail. This board is a twin style outline hence the 69 reference to Siamese as the width in the nose and tail are the same. I69 is a chopped down version of the Automatic meant to have a low swing weight for rotations and air surfing. This board reminds us of a low profile wakeboard shape tuned up for surfing. Stock Dims: 5'2" x 19.25" x 2.38" x 26.7L 5'5" x 19.38" x 2.44" x 28.9L 5'6" x 19.5" x 2.5" x 30.2L 5'7" x 19.63" x 2.56" x 31.6L (Albee's Size) 5'8" x 19.88" x 2.6" x 33L 5'9" x 20" x 2.63" x 34L 5'10" x 20.25" x 2.75" x 36.5L 6'0" x 20.5" x 2.88" x 39.8L Custom Order *We can custom build to your specs 6-8 week turnaround The team at Infinity is dedicated to providing the best customer service and high 5's to all our customers. Have a question about a board? What size to select? Thinking of going custom? Give us a call or email Dave@infinitysurf.com and work on your design direct! We look forward to chatting with you! description: The GRX is an extension of the Automatic. The Automatic is our flagship high performance design which really comes alive in good to better waves. Good surfers can make it work in small stuff as well but we were finding that most people wanted the performance gains from the design but tuned to everyday conditions. An Automatic step down so to speak. This is where the GRX comes in. Dan Boehne took the baseline of his Automatic and approached that DNA with an everyday shortboard design in the GRX. The rockers are the same, nice and curvy, but the GRX features more generous dims and a deeper concave to flatten the center rocker line to help lift over flat sections and generating speed with less effort. One of the unique design features is the GRX has a "rolled" step rail which in effect takes volume away from the rail line (where you don't want it) and puts it back in the center. This allows to achieve the same thin rail apex from your normal board in the water while turning, but keep or "hide" the volume in the middle (where you need it) for paddle power and efficient volume ratio. Standard Dims: 5'6" x 18.5" x 2.25" x 24.5L 5'7" x 18.63" x 2.28" x 25.5L 5'8" x 18.75" x 2.32" x 26.50L 5'9" x 19" x 2.32" x 27.25L 5'10" x 19.25" x 2.35" x 28.50L 5'11" x 19.50" x 2.38 x 29.50L 6'0" x 19.75" x 2.45" x 31L

description: This is our medium-big guy performance shortboard. Intended for the larger surfer who surfs good. Same characteristics as the Automatic with some tweaks to help the bigger guy. We move the outline and thickness a bit forward under the chest to aid in wave catching and front foot style driving down the line. This board has a unique "rolled" step rail to provide a thin rail in the water when turning. We take the volume away from the rail and "hide" it in the deck where you need it for paddle power and balanced volume distribution. Available from 6'0" - 6'10". Dan is 5'10" and 210lbs he rides a 6'2" Silverback listed are some standard dims to set a baseline. We can custom adjust if you want to get specific. Standard Dimensions: 6' x 20 1/2" x 2 1/2" (35.9L) 6'2" x 20 3/4" x 2 5/8" (38L) 6'4" x 21" x 2 3/4" (41.5L) 6'6" x 21 1/4" x 2 7/8" x (45L) 6'8" x 21 3/4" x 3" (48.7L) 6'10"x 22" x 3 1/4" (55.4L)

description: One of our most popular models of all time. A must have in the quiver. With the 5F there are no more excuses for not surfing on even the smallest crappiest days. This board really is that much fun! This board has tons of volume allowing guys to ride a very short board. This makes the board extremely quick and loose. Exactly what you want when the waves are tiny and/or mushy. 5F means 5 fins. We like the quad set up on extreme small days and the thruster when the waves get a bit bigger or when you want more pivot. Don't be afraid to go small we recommend going 6-8" inches smaller than your standard thruster. Available from 5'2" to 6'4". Standard Dimensions 5'2" x 19.75" x 2.65" x 29.6L 5'4" x 19.88" x 2.75" x 32L 5'6" x 20" x 2.75" x 33.1L 5'8" x 20.25" x 2.75" x 34.8L 5'10" x 20.5" x 2.88" x 37.8L 6' x 21" x 2.88" x 39.9L 6'2" x 21.5" x 3" x 43.5L

description: This is a thinner, narrower, step up version of our famed 5F model. People love the 5F so much they tend to ride it all the time, including when the conditions might not be the best match. Compared to the 5F, the High5 has a thinner foil and curvier rocker. The tail features a step in the outline behind the bump to quickly take out unwanted volume for response and bite. Ride this board 4-6" shorter than standard. Many 5F lovers have been replacing the standard short board with this model. Size this board approx 4-6" shorter, 1/2-3/4" wider and keep the same thickness as your standard performance shortboard. Below are some standard dims to give you a base. We can custom order specific sizing. Dave Boehne is 5'10" and 160lbs. He rides the 5'4" x 19 1/4" x 2 1/4" High5 in the video below. description: Classic round nosed fish design. This style fish has been a staple in many surfboard companies lines for years now due to its versatility. The board features a wider round nose for small waves and has a shortboard back half enabling it to handle some juice. This latest version has a wider tail block than a shortboard for that glide in between sections. We've added 4 deep channels for controlling the added speed. Below is a list of standard Dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust dims if you want to get specific with the design. Standard Dimensions: 5'4" x 18.4" x 2.2" (24.7L) 5'6" x 18.9" x 2.3" (27.9L) 5'8" x 19.5" x 2.4" (29.6L) 5'10"x 20" x 2.4" (31.8L) 6' x 20.7" x 2.5" (35L) 6'2" x 21.25" x 2.6" (38L) 6'4" x 21.8" x 2.7" (41L)

description: Short and stubby step-down board. A team favorite because it's easy to transition riding when coming off a standard short board. Wide chopped off square tail and a deep single concave. We added a wide exit compression channel that forms a touch of vee out the tail that helps keep momentum out of turns. Riding these smaller boards allows you to fit into more critical parts of the wave while maintaining the same volume. Ride this board 4-6" shorter than standard. Below are some standard dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust if you want to get specific. Standard Dimensions 5'4" x 19" x 2 1/8" (23L) 5'6" x 19 1/4" x 2 1/4" (26L) 5'8" x 19 3/4" x 2 3/8" (29L) 5'10" x 20" x 2 1/2" (32L) 6' x 20 1/4" x 2 5/8" (35L) 6'2" x 20 1/2" x 2 3/4" (38L) 6'4" x 20 3/4" x 2 7/8" (41L)

description: Rainmaker is a performance style fish that was designed for fast down the line speed, full out rail carving and multi fin versatility. This board features low pinched rails and a flat rocker for speed and precise edge control. We wanted to make a modern performance fish design that could work as a thruster, quad, or twin depending on the approach of the surfer. This board either comes standard as a 5 fin with double wings or is set as a twin with single wing like you see in the photo. A trailer box can be ordered custom as well. Standard Dims: 5'4" x 19" x 2.30" x 26.50L 5'5" x 19.25" x 2.32" x 27.50L 5'6" x 19.50" x 2.35" x 28.75L 5'7" x 19.75" x 2.35" x 29.75L 5'8" x 20" x 2.40" x 31L 5'9" x 20.25" x 2.44" x 32.50L 5'10" x 20.50" x 2.50" x 34L 5'11" x 20.75" x 2.55" x 35.50L 6' x 21" x 2.60" x 37L 6'2" x 21.50" x 2.70" x 40.50L 6'4" x 21.75" x 2.75" x 43L

description: High volume with low angular rails and progressive single to double concave. This bottom matched up with the wide round tail and quad fin allow this board to go very fast but also come off the bottom with more traction and purpose. This board loves to go fast, carve that open face, and catch waves. Can be custom ordered with a semi point nose or round nose for preferred aesthetics. description: This mini-Simmons style twin is easily the fastest planing surf vessel we've ever ridden. Minimal effort get's this board flying down the line. Lots of stuff going on here. A parallel outline provides less drag with twin-fin keels. The deck features a blended deck providing a thin nose and tail while maintaining the volume under the chest for paddling and a thin angular rail that meets the water. The bottom has a rolled hull entry for planing that blends into a single concave exit. Standard Dims: 4'10" x 21" x 2.65" x 32.3L 5'0" x 21.25" x 2.65" x 33.8L 5'2" x 21.25" x 2.75" x 36.3L 5'4" x 21.5" x 2.88" x 39.7L 5'6"x 21.65" x 2.88" x 40.9L 5'8" x 21.75" x 2.88" x 42.8L 5'10" x 22" x 2.88" x 44.4L 6' x 22.25" x 2.88" x 46.3L

description: Traditional twin fin keel fish Still one of the most fun rides around. Flat rocker and giant swallow tail. You know the deal. We've added some modern touches to the classic. There is a slight concave in the deck that bleeds out to a moderate angled rail. This allows high volume and performance while maintaining the integrity of the classic fish ride. In general, we like to build these boards with resin tints and high-end finishes but can custom basics like clear sanded as well. Below is a list of standard Dims to give you a baseline. We can custom adjust dims if you want to get specific with the design. Standard Dimensions 4'10" x 19" x 2 3/8" 5' x 19 1/2" x 2 3/8" 5'2" x 19 3/4" x 2 1/2" 5'4" x 20" x 2 1/2" 5'6" x 20 1/2" x 2 5/8" 5'8" x 21" x 2 5/8" 5'10" x 21 1/2" x 2 3/4" 6" x 22" x 2 7/8"

description: Speed Egg is our classic 2+1 mid length Dave Boehne design. This board has been around for decades but we've modernized it and expanded it's range of waves people are riding these in. Great wave catcher and lots of performance off the tail. Still has that mid face high line glide with a little cheater five nose navigation built in as well. The bottom has a slight rolled entry for quick lift that blends in to a mellow single concave that leads in to the deep vee concave in the sweet spot. This board has that great combination of down the line trim and rail to rail carve. Standard Dims: 6'10" x 20.8" x 2.65" x 40.5L 7' x 21.3" x 2.75" x 43.6L 7'2" x 21.8" x 2.88" x 46.8L 7'6" x 22" x 2.88" x 51.7L 7'10" x 22.5" x 3" x 57.7L ***we can and have made this board shorter as well as longer as custom orders***

description: It has become one of our most popular models simply because no other manufacturer has had the foresight to copy it, if you want it, you can only get it from us. I developed this model for myself because as I grew older, heavier and busier, I felt that my thin high performance boards were just getting too hard to paddle. I still surfed as good as ever and I wanted to maintain that fast, zippy feel of a shorter board without being forced to ride a long glide-in board all the time. I started adding thickness to my 8' funboard shape. For my weight, I found that I could make the board 3.75 thick and it would still perform fantastically, but it was so much easier to paddle and catch waves on. Now I shape the Secret Weapon from 8 to 10 and up to 4.25 thick for really big guys. Standard Dimensions 7'6" x 23" x 3 3/4" 8' x 23" x 3 7/8" 8'6" x 24' x 4" 9' x 24" x 4 1/4" The Original Secret Weapon I started making this model around the time that I turned 40. I had gained about 15 lb. And didn't have time to surf as much, but I still surfed good as ever (so I thought). I loved that off the bottom and straight up the face surfing style that you can only get on an 8 board, but I just needed more floatation and wave catchability, so I started making my fun boards 3.75 thick. Soon my friends and customers were ordering these boards and the Secret Weapon model was born in 1988. In the early 90's we discovered the powerful Cluster tri-fin set up which gave the Secret Weapon even better performance. The 18.25" wide nose Secret Weapon template is only available from an EPS/epoxy blank, but the semi pointed template is available in traditional polyester or EPS/epoxy. The Secret Rad Noserider From popular demand, I created this model from our famous Rad Noserider. It has the same half-length nose concave and sweet spot rocker along with a 20 nose and 16 tail, which adds even more to wave catchability. Because of the shorter length, it is even easier to nose ride simply because you are much closer to the nose. This is the most fun small wave board I have ever ridden. The board featured has our traction pad and clear Monster Spray on the nose. The pad keeps your board clean, offers the best traction and is very comfortable to knee paddle on. The Quad fin model With this model, I have maximized performance by using a shortboard single concave and rocker and a more pulled in nose. It still has outstanding wave catchability because of the width and thickness. The bottom rocker is very advanced because the rail line maintains a normal curvy rocker that assists powerful rail turns, but the planing surface has a concave in the center of the board, which flattens out the rocker for ballistic trim speed. The concave also moves the rocker curve back towards the tail to a point right between your feet. At this point, the concave transitions into a V-bottom. It's like standing on a ball bearing when you turn. This bottom contour goes faster and turns better than a regular bottom. For those who prefer a wider nose, you can custom order a quad fin bottom contour with an original Secret Weapon wide nose outline, or with three fins instead of four. The narrow nosed quad template is available in normal Polyester or EPS/ Epoxy, which is about 20% lighter, but also about 20% more expensive. Semi Pointed The only blank available in polyester now does not have a wide enough nose for the original template, so we use a semi pointed 17.5 nose for all the poly Boards. Many guys who don't nose ride prefer this more drawn in template anyway. Custom Order Infinity SUP / Surfboards Showroom The team at Infinity Surf is dedicated to providing the best customer service and high 5's to all our customers. Have a question about a board? What size to select? Have questions about our company? Just send us a note or give us a call and we look forward to chatting with you! 24382 Del Prado Dana Point, CA 92629 (949) 661-0578

For those who prefer a wider nose, you can custom order a quad fin bottom contour with an original Secret Weapon wide nose outline, or with three fins instead of four. The narrow nosed quad template is available in normal Polyester or EPS/ Epoxy, which is about 20% lighter, but also about 20% more expensive. Semi Pointed The only blank available in polyester now does not have a wide enough nose for the original template, so we use a semi pointed 17.5 nose for all the poly Boards. Many guys who don't nose ride prefer this more drawn in template anyway. Now I shape the Secret Weapon from 8 to 10 and up to 4.25 thick for really big guys. description: The Competitor is our state of the art high-performance longboard for the progressive shortboard style of longboarding. The concept was developed by infinity and ridden to many victories. This design has graced the cover of LONGBOARD Magazine multiple times. Back in the day, Taylor Jenson and The Robbins brothers (Cole and Brett) have won many longboard competitions all over the world including the US Open of Longboarding on this very design. Since then, we have continued to evolve the Competitor with an updated design and bottom contours. These boards are thin, light, and extremely responsive. If you are looking for a pro-level competition-style longboard, look no further. description: The Retro Rad Noserider has all the same characteristics of the original with some specific design elements to compliment the "retro" feel We flattened the rocker a bit but kept the signature Rad entry nose concave and bevels. We added a more 60/40 style rail to give the board smooth and forgiving trimming and face climbing. The tail is pulled in to a semi wide "hip" squash or pin. This board has the feel of a classic nose rider but with hidden design to give it some performance. Turn easier and walk around on the deck with more stability. Below are some standard dims we generally stock. We can custom if you want to get specific. Standard Dimensions 9' x 22" x 3 1/4" 9'3" x 22 1/2" x 3 1/4" 9'6" x 23" x 3 1/2" 9'9" x 23 1/2" x 3 5/8"

description: The "Modern" Classic was designed with the timeless feels of predictable acceleration and smooth, flowing turns that our original Classic board has but updated with a thinner rail profile and a slight tucked edge out the tail. This version has a thinner 60/40 rail that still offer a forgiving, natural-feeling ride while adding some technical precision while trimming. Stable walking up and down the board, locked in nose rides and flowing turns off the tail. Typical Dims: 9' x 22.5" x 2.5" 9'6 x 23" x 2.65" 10' x 23.5" x 3"

description: The Hook Rail is an archived model from the 80's era originally designed by Steve Boehne and Terry Senate. The "hook" described the unique bottom rail shape that grips through turns and rail transitions at the tail wing. This model was a team favorite and one of our most popular boards in the early thruster era. For 2022 Dave Boehne took the original templates and spec'd out one of his original boards from his wall collection and reimagined this design for today's modern era without sacrificing the Hook Rail's original magic. This board has a very moderate rocker throughout starting with a slow flowing entry rocker and continuing through a smooth constant curve and leaving the tail with a low exit rocker out the tail keeping this board super fast, predictable and forgiving for every day conditions. The bottom shape has a slight vee that enters the board on entry which gives the flatter rocker entry more play. This vee carries down the middle of the board as it hits the wing the "hooked" bottom edge is introduced and complimented by a deep vee in the sweet spot which gives this board the magic combo of rail to rail transition with grip. This vee continues out the tailblock behind the fin. The deck and outline have the 80's influenced flatter deck and boxy rail that has a moderate angle which leads down to the tucked edges. The outline has a slightly narrower nose compared to a modern shortboard with a wing in front of the fins to free up the wide tail. This board has a lot of get up and go and the wider tail keeps pace in flat spots of the wave. Original shortboard Hook Rails were ridden longer and narrower but these are designed for more everyday surf conditions. This board could kind of be categorized as a groveler shortboard but really it has a much wider range than that. Match up your volume or your small wave volume and you are good to go. STOCK DIMS: 5'6" x 18.88" x 2.25" x 25.5L 5'8" x 19.3" x 2.38" x 27.8L 5'10" x 19.88" x 2.44" x 30.3L 6' x 20.5" x 2.44" x 33.2L 6'2" x 21" x 2.5" x 35.9L ***We can custom and build to spec as well***

For 2022 Dave Boehne took the original templates and spec'd out one of his original boards from his wall collection and reimagined this design for today's modern era without sacrificing the Hook Rail's original magic. This board has a very moderate rocker throughout starting with a slow flowing entry rocker and continuing through a smooth constant curve and leaving the tail with a low exit rocker out the tail keeping this board super fast, predictable and forgiving for every day conditions. The bottom shape has a slight vee that enters the board on entry which gives the flatter rocker entry more play. This vee carries down the middle of the board as it hits the wing the "hooked" bottom edge is introduced and complimented by a deep vee in the sweet spot which gives this board the magic combo of rail to rail transition with grip. This vee continues out the tailblock behind the fin. The deck and outline have the 80's influenced flatter deck and boxy rail that has a moderate angle which leads down to the tucked edges. The outline has a slightly narrower nose compared to a modern shortboard with a wing in front of the fins to free up the wide tail. This board has a lot of get up and go and the wider tail keeps pace in flat spots of the wave. Original shortboard Hook Rails were ridden longer and narrower but these are designed for more everyday surf conditions. This board could kind of be categorized as a groveler shortboard but really it has a much wider range than that. Match up your volume or your small wave volume and you are good to go. STOCK DIMS: 5'6" x 18.88" x 2.25" x 25.5L 5'8" x 19.3" x 2.38" x 27.8L 5'10" x 19.88" x 2.44" x 30.3L 6' x 20.5" x 2.44" x 33.2L 6'2" x 21" x 2.5" x 35.9L ***We can custom and build to spec as well***

description: RPCT "Round Pin Channeled Twin" Mid length twin fin model. With the rising popularity in mid-length shapes there are many versions of this board out there and this is our design version. Great wave catcher, fast trim speed, and lots of adjustment between friction free feels and drive off the tail with different fins. Lots of versatility in this shape. This board has a virtual "twin" outline as the nose is only .5" wider than the tail. Moderate over all rocker curve with no flat spot. Still has a nice "kick" in nose and tail. This rocker plan enables a board of this size to be loose and noble for its length while keeping that high line trim. The bottom is a combination of this rocker matched up with a simple vee bottom in which we place specific channels. We "spine" the center stringer in the channels acting like an elongated stabilizer between the fins and match that up with nice tucked and thin rails. We like this board best in the 6'10" - 7'6" range but have made a bunch both longer as well as shortboard sizes under 6' all depends what you are after. Stock Sizes: 6'6" x 20.5" x 2.65" x 37L 6'10" x 20.88" x 2.75" x 42L 7'2" x 21.25" x 2.88" x 47L 7'6" x 21.75" x 2.88" x 51L 7'10" x 22" x 3 x 57L

description: This is designed off an old archived model called the "Slinger" we started making about 11 years ago! Slinger was a small wave shortboard that had a bit wider nose and mid section but pulled in quickly with a noticeable "bump" in the outline that finished off with a small narrow swallow tail. The board was a fast small wave board and kept control in better or slightly bigger waves. With the love of the twin fin back in a big way, this model was the perfect baseline to make a performance driven twinny. We modernized it and added a wing in place of the previous bump, dropped it further back towards the tail to match up with the pivot point and placement of the twin fin set up. This straightened the outline in the back half of the board which works well with twins and adds that wider surface area at the fins for excellent speed. The wing breaks right behind the fins and abruptly narrows the tail with lower rails which both add up to more control which is needed with faster boards. What you get is a very fast, razor sharp twin with confidence off the bottom like a thruster but have all that free'd up spark a twin provides. Stock Dimensions: ***custom dims available*** 5'3" x 18.9" x 2 5/16" x 24.7L 5'5" x 19.25" x 2.3" x 26.8L 5'7" x 19.5" x 2.5" x 29.3L 5'9" x 19.75" x 2.75" x 32.7L 5'11" x 20.25" x 2.75" x 34.5L 6'2" x 21" x 2.88" x 39.7L

We modernized it and added a wing in place of the previous bump, dropped it further back towards the tail to match up with the pivot point and placement of the twin fin set up. This straightened the outline in the back half of the board which works well with twins and adds that wider surface area at the fins for excellent speed. The wing breaks right behind the fins and abruptly narrows the tail with lower rails which both add up to more control which is needed with faster boards. What you get is a very fast, razor sharp twin with confidence off the bottom like a thruster but have all that free'd up spark a twin provides. description: Diamond Mind Spaceship Twin This mid-length - higher volume performance twin fin shape is a fun collaboration between Dave Boehne and Eric Diamond. Eric Diamond is a long time Infinity friend and one of the original artist/creatives in the industry. "ED" was a big part of the competition team back in the 1980's and even worked the surf shop floors as a grommet! To come back full circle and design a board that fits his signature rail and flow style was a ton of fun and the design is one that will fit a lot of surfers needs. Incredible wave catching capabilities, down the line speed, high line trim and full rail cutbacks. The Spaceship has a slight wide point forward outline which throws the volume distro more under the chest and front foot which adds both paddle power and front foot down the line speed. The tail pulls in at the twin fins and creates that sure footed pivot point where the upright twins engage and bit into the turn. ED likes the added "guitar pick" trailer fin to help control the transitions from rail to rail. The deck is flat with an abrupt angled rail throwing all the volume in the middle of the board where you want it and thinning the rail allowing for sensitive and precise carves. This design is a perfect blend of the high volume we all desire with the performance we want to keep. Don't be afraid to catch more waves! Volume is your friend. STANDARD DIMS: 6'6" x 21 x 2.75" x 44.02L 6'11" x 22" x 3" x 53.78L 7'4" x 22.25" x 3.25" x 63L 8' x 22.25" x 3.25" x 67.59L ***can adjust dims for custom orders

description: The "InfinFLEX" version is an EPS core which is vacuum bagged with a Carbon Fiber and PVC "hybrid" construction offering a very light, durable and custom tuned flex pattern. The Rad Noserider has been quoted: my favorite board ever by thousands of surfers. The deep half-length nose concave creates incredible nose rides and the sweet spot rocker with aggressive V-bottom offers smooth zigzag turns. This board has beveled rails around the concave for edge control, tucked edge rails for fast response and our famous Cluster Fin set up for projection out of bottom turns. We offer the fins as a convertible set up to switch from 2+1 to Cluster to Single fin for ultimate versatility. Our hand-shaped boards are available in polyester or eps/epoxy and in any length or thickness. Standard Dims: 9' x 22" x 66L 9'6" x 23" x 76L 10' x 23.5" 82L

description: Infinity Pocket Rocket shaped by Steve Boehne. 6'11" x 21.5" x 2.65" Standard PU construction with air spray design top and bottom and FUTURES boxes. The Pocket Rocket was originally designed by Steve Boehne in the 70's. While we were moving our shaping factory to a new location, we came across a box way in the back shelf that had a bunch of our original board logos. A miscellaneous collection of archived designs. With it being 2020 and the timing of us celebrating our 50th Anniversary as a company, we thought it would be fun to re-issue some of the old designs and make them with the original throw back logos. Turns out the Pocket Rocket has been our most exciting design and best seller so we've expanded it into it's own series. The Pocket Rocket, The Pocket Mini, The Pocket Quad, The Pocket Twin, and the Pocket Single. All boards build off of the same design platform but tweaked for the chosen fin configuration. The Pocket Rocket-Mini design is a rad ultra short peformance groveler. The idea is to ride it as short as possible in a high volume so its fast, compact, with tons of float to fly over sections. The rocker is flatter than the standard Pocket Rocket but still has that signature "kick" in the nose. The outline is short and stubby so its super loose and skatey rail to rail. The 80's inspired "tucked" edge style rails give it down the line speed along with edge control in speed turns. Still has the same tail and bottom as the original so the cluster of the fins matched with the channels.

description: 5F is our staple groveler shape that has been in our lineup since 2011! high volume short performance board. 5 fins to have some versatility and fine tuning to different conditions. It's a proven shape that has lasted the test of time. We always seem to keep going back to the 5F when looking for a tried and true everyday surf shape! The "InfinFLEX" version is an EPS core which is vacuum bagged with a Carbon Fiber and PVC "hybrid" construction offering a very light, durable and custom tuned flex pattern. Standard Dimensions: 5'2" x 19.75" x 2.65" x 29.6L 5'6" x 20" x 2.75" x 33.1L 5'10"x 20.5" x 2.88" x 37.8L 6'2" x 21.5" x 3" x 43.5L Video Construction

Standard Dimensions: 5'2" x 19.75" x 2.65" x 29.6L 5'6" x 20" x 2.75" x 33.1L 5'10"x 20.5" x 2.88" x 37.8L 6'2" x 21.5" x 3" x 43.5L Video Construction

description: Rainmaker is a performance style fish that was designed for fast down the line speed, full out rail carving and multi fin versatility. This board features low pinched rails and a flat rocker for speed and precise edge control. We wanted to make a modern performance fish design that could work as a thruster, quad, or twin depending on the approach of the surfer. This board either comes standard as a 5 fin with double wings or is set as a twin with single wing like you see in the photo. A trailer box can be ordered custom as well. description: Infinity Rad Noserider shaped by Steve Boehne. 9'0" x 23" x 2 3/4" This one features a nice air spray on the bottom with an inlay red In the concave. Wraps up to the deck and a black pin line separates the clear deck. Futures single fin and matte sanded finish. The Rad Noserider has been quoted: my favorite board ever by thousands of surfers. The deep half-length nose concave creates incredible nose rides and the sweet spot rocker with aggressive V-bottom offers smooth zigzag turns. This board has beveled rails around the concave for edge control, tucked edge rails for fast response and our famous Cluster Fin set up for projection out of bottom turns. We offer the fins as a convertible set up to switch from 2+1 to Cluster to Single fin for ultimate versatility. Our hand-shaped boards are available in polyester or eps/epoxy and in any length or thickness.
